summary: Decompilation pass for src/melee/gm/gm_19EF, replacing major stubs around the gm_19EF game-mode scene setup and OnEnter path with C implementations and typed globals. The diff implements helpers for animation/state updates, loads GmGover/GmGoCoin/GmGoAnim/GmRgStnd assets, initializes HSD camera/light/JObj/SIS text objects, and updates headers for concrete prototypes. decomp.dev reported 5 new matches and large improvements to the remaining gm_19EF functions, though there were no human review comments in the slice.
key_files: src/melee/gm/gm_19EF.c - main decomp work: globals, helpers, fn_8019F9C4, gm_801A0A10_OnEnter
src/melee/gm/gm_19EF.h - fn_8019F9C4 prototype changed from UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS to void(u32)
src/melee/gm/gm_1601.c - fn_80168F2C gained an ignored s8 parameter
src/melee/gm/gm_1601.h - fn_80168F2C declaration updated to void(s8)
what_changed: Expanded gm_19EF.c from stubs into a 600+ line implementation, adding static helpers fn_8019EFC4, fn_8019F1D0, fn_8019F2D4, fn_8019F6EC, and fn_8019F810.

Implemented fn_8019F9C4(u32), which selects a character index by gm_801A4310/MJ_CLASSIC/MJ_ADVENTURE/MJ_ALLSTAR, loads scene/model archives, creates camera/light/model GObjs, attaches JObjs, initializes animation frames, SIS text, and audio cues.

Implemented gm_801A0A10_OnEnter(void*) by casting to DebugGameOverData, initializing lbl_80479A98 fields, capping a u16 value at 0x3E7, computing a decrement step over 120 frames, storing slot/character data, and calling fn_8019F9C4(arg0->ckind).
Replaced raw padding in static lbl_80479A98 with typed offset fields: state bytes, counters, HSD_Text*, HSD_GObj*, an array of ten HSD_JObj* entries, digit-animation JObjs, and remaining pad_XX gaps.

Added typed static globals for SceneDesc pointers, HSD_Joint/HSD_JObj pointers, and small { HSD_JObj* x0; s32 x4; } animation-state structs at lbl_804D66B0, lbl_804D66C0, lbl_804D66C8, lbl_804D66D0, and lbl_804D66E0.
Changed fn_80168F2C from void(void) to void(s8 ignored), matching the new gm_19EF callsite fn_80168F2C(0) while leaving the function body behavior unchanged.
Updated gm_19EF.h so fn_8019F9C4 is declared as void fn_8019F9C4(u32) instead of an unknown return/parameter placeholder.
Added required includes for gm_1601, gm_1A3F, gm_1A45, it/inlines.h, controller, dobj, mobj, lb_00B0, toy, tylist, and sislib.
smart_moves: Used typed globals and offset-named fields instead of opaque padding, which made the .bss item fully match according to the bot report.
Kept semantics conservative: most unresolved symbols retained address-style names, while only prototypes and types directly evidenced by callsites were made concrete.
Matched scene setup by preserving the HSD object creation sequence: archive loads, camera CObj, light LObj, JObj load, GX links, setup procs, animation request, then animation application.
Mode-specific character lookup was handled explicitly through gm_801A4310 and MJ_CLASSIC/MJ_ADVENTURE/MJ_ALLSTAR, with fallback char_kind 8 when gm_80160474 returned -1.
The number-to-string helper fn_8019F1D0 uses manual digit counting and remainder division rather than library formatting, a common codegen-friendly tactic for matching retail code.
Adjusted fn_80168F2C's signature to accept an unused s8 parameter instead of forcing a no-argument declaration, aligning the header with observed ABI/callsite evidence.
decomp_lessons: Large game-mode scene functions often become matchable by first reconstructing their static state block and HSD asset-loading pipeline, even if semantic field names are still unknown.
When new callsites expose a real argument list, update shared headers immediately; otherwise downstream decomp code may need casts or mismatched prototypes.
For UI/scene code, matching depends heavily on exact ordering of HSD_JObjReqAnimAll/HSD_JObjAnimAll, HSD_MObjReqAnim/HSD_MObjAnim, flag toggles, audio calls, and GObj proc setup.
Offset-field structs with pad_XX names are useful intermediate documentation: they preserve layout while giving future PRs anchors for real names.
Partial high-percent matches are still valuable in this project: the bot showed fn_8019F9C4 at 97.19%, fn_8019F2D4 at 99.48%, and fn_8019EFC4 at 98.38% while smaller helpers fully matched.
The PR body and review comments were sparse; most durable evidence comes from the diff and decomp.dev report, not from reviewer explanation.
naming_conventions: Unresolved functions and labels retain address-style names such as fn_8019F9C4, fn_8019F2D4, lbl_80479A98, and lbl_804D66C8.
Game-mode lifecycle naming uses the existing pattern gm_801A0A10_OnEnter and gm_801A0B18_OnLeave.
Struct fields in unknown static state use offset names x0, x4, x8, x1C, x2C, etc., with explicit padding names such as pad_01, pad_17, pad_21, pad_61, and pad_74.
Header declarations keep address comments, e.g. /* 19F9C4 */ void fn_8019F9C4(u32);
Unused-but-required parameters can be named ignored, as in void fn_80168F2C(s8 ignored).
Known enum-like mode constants are preferred where available: MJ_CLASSIC, MJ_ADVENTURE, and MJ_ALLSTAR.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used PAD_STACK(16) in fn_8019F810 and PAD_STACK(8) in fn_8019F9C4 to shape stack frames.
Preserved explicit casts to u8, u16, s8, u32, and f32 around comparisons, arithmetic, function arguments, and animation-frame requests.
Kept repeated null-check child/next traversal instead of abstracting it, matching branch structure for JObj tree navigation.
Used reverse loops over i = 10 down to 1 for coin/JObj flag updates, setting or clearing flag 0x10 based on lbl_80479A98.x70.
Kept local nested scopes for variables such as game_mode, val, and loop indices, which can affect register allocation and lifetime.
Used GET_JOBJ(gobj) via it/inlines.h in fn_8019F6EC.
Used a function-pointer cast in HSD_GObj_SetupProc(gobj, (void (*)(HSD_GObj*)) fn_8019F810, 0) because fn_8019F810 is declared void(void).
Left a documented TODO around cam_gobj->gx_link = (u8) 0x801 because the assigned value does not fit the apparent u8 field, preserving current matching behavior while flagging a type issue.
review_feedback: No human review comments were present in the provided slice.
PR body only said: "Tracking PR as usual".
decomp.dev reported matched code 62.24% (+0.03%, +1252 bytes) and matched data 37.89% (+0.01%, +120 bytes) for GALE01.
decomp.dev listed 5 new matches: fn_8019F810 (+436), fn_8019F6EC (+292), gm_801A0A10_OnEnter (+264), fn_8019F1D0 (+260), and gm_19EF .bss (+60).
decomp.dev listed improvements in unmatched items: fn_8019F9C4 to 97.19%, fn_8019F2D4 to 99.48%, fn_8019EFC4 to 98.38%, plus .data/.sbss/.sdata2 improvements.
The bot also reported a small .sdata regression of -2 bytes, from 56.52% to 51.52%.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs touching gm_19EF.c to see whether fn_8019F9C4, fn_8019F2D4, and fn_8019EFC4 reached 100% and whether their remaining mismatches were documented.
Search for renames of lbl_80479A98 fields or DebugGameOverData fields to recover semantic names for x0/x8/x1C/x1E/x20/x22.
Search for fixes to the TODO: cam_gobj->gx_link = (u8) 0x801, because it suggests the gx_link field type or assignment needs correction.
Search for additional callers or later signature changes of fn_80168F2C(s8) to determine whether the ignored parameter has real meaning.
Search gm_19EF asset-name PRs involving GmGover.dat, GmGoCoin.dat, GmGoAnim.dat, GmRgStnd.dat, SdIntro.dat, or SIS_IntroData for better scene semantics.
